fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Wisconsin Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Wisconsin - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity wisconsin, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to wisconsin, speech-language pathologist compact wisconsin, wisconsin speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Examining Board of Speech-Language Pathologists and Audiologists
board.parent_dept: Wisconsin Department of Safety and Professional Services (DSPS)
board.mailing_address:
Department of Safety and Professional Services
P.O. Box 8935
Madison, WI 53708-8935

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: Full credential for independent SLP practice in Wisconsin.

license_types.title: Provisional SLP License
license_types.description: For SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

steps.title: Create Account and Submit Online Application
steps.description: Access the Wisconsin DSPS Online Licensing Gateway at https://licenses.wi.gov/, register for an account (if you don't have one), and complete the online application for a Speech-Language Pathologist license.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the required application fee through the Wisconsin DSPS Online Licensing Gateway during the online application process.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts, confirming a master's or higher degree in Speech-Language Pathology, to be sent directly from your educational institution to the Wisconsin DSPS.

steps.title: Provide Verification of Experience and Praxis Scores
steps.description: Provide documentation of supervised clinical experience (e.g., ASHA Certificate of Clinical Competence in Speech-Language Pathology - CCC-SLP) and ensure your official Praxis examination scores in Speech-Language Pathology are sent directly to the Wisconsin DSPS from Educational Testing Service (ETS).

steps.title: Request Out-of-State License Verification (for Endorsement)
steps.description: If licensed in other states, request official verification of all current and past Speech-Language Pathologist licenses held to be sent directly from the respective licensing boards to the Wisconsin DSPS.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check the status of your application and any outstanding requirements by logging into your account on the Wisconsin DSPS Online Licensing Gateway.

telepractice_text: Wisconsin permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.
provisional_text: Wisconsin issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: Wisconsin is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

faqs.question: How much does a Wisconsin SLP license cost?
Is Wisconsin part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does Wisconsin allow telepractice for SLPs?
Does Wisconsin have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Wisconsin participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Wisconsin without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Wisconsin?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Wisconsin while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Wisconsin is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in Wisconsin
hero.dek: Applicants for a Speech-Language Pathologist license in Wisconsin must apply online through the Wisconsin DSPS Online Licensing Gateway.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps:
1. Go to the Wisconsin DSPS Online Licensing Gateway (https://licenses.wi.gov/).
2. Click 'Register for an Account' to create a new user ID and password, or log in if you already have an account.
3. Once logged in, select 'Start a New Application' from the menu.
4. Follow the prompts to select 'Speech-Language Pathologist' as your desired credential type and complete the application.
